pairs.question: What is the title awarded to the top ensemble at the end of the Drum Corps International (DCI) World Class season?
pairs.answer: The title awarded is the DCI World Class Champion (formerly known as the DCI Division I World Champion).

pairs.question: How many rounds make up the DCI World Class championships, and what are their names?
pairs.answer: The DCI World Class championships consist of three rounds: Preliminaries, Semifinals, and Finals.

pairs.question: What was the format change for DCI World Class corps starting in the 2011 season?
pairs.answer: Starting in 2011, all active DCI corps (both Open and World Class) were permitted to compete for the championship. Previously, only World Class corps competed, with a smaller number advancing through Quarterfinals.

pairs.question: How many World Class corps competed in the Preliminaries of the DCI championships as of 2024?
pairs.answer: As of 2024, approximately 40 World Class corps compete in the Preliminaries.

pairs.question: What were the special circumstances surrounding the 2020 and 2021 DCI World Championship seasons due to the COVID-19 pandemic?
pairs.answer: In 2020, the DCI World Championship and tour were cancelled entirely. In 2021, the season resumed without scores, and it concluded with a three-day exhibition performance event at Lucas Oil Stadium.

pairs.question: List the active DCI World Class corps that have won the championship title the most times.
pairs.answer: The corps with the most titles are: Blue Devils (21 titles), The Cadets (10 titles), The Cavaliers (7 titles), and Santa Clara Vanguard (7 titles).

pairs.question: Which DCI World Class corps are no longer active?
pairs.answer: The corps no longer active are The Anaheim Kingsmen, Star of Indiana, and The Cadets.
